item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image originates from here, which requires buying pictures for use. As such, it is not compliant with CC 3.0 and has been removed.

item.title: Just a clock that makes wizards
item.content:encoded:
I came from SCP-1466!

I don't like how you somehow connected an anomalous clock which perpetually almost reaches 8 o'clock to reality benders with powers only marginally related to the overall theme of time. There's so much wasted potential here, and yet you make it all about Mary Sues.

Disappointed. - 1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The whole "halves once per hour" bit really doesn't make that much sense when you consider how quickly that'd fall under Planck length.

Overall, I feel these are too many things piled up to really make the article enjoyable. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heya author! I went to edit the word "an" in the article (before "analog") and uncovered a draft that was saved on the edit screen but not on the article itself. I reverted back, but if that was your edit and not someone else's, it's there for posterity. You should save by pressing "save" and not "Save Draft".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe it has a sticker that says "Warning: Cognitohazard" and a regular locker doesn't. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a lot of stuff going on here and I don't think it really coheres into a single interesting story that well. You could probably cut… most of the stuff with the D-class and not really lose anything.

SCP-2441 is to be kept within a standard Cognitohazard Containment Locker.

You describe things as "standard" way too often for me to take seriously, especially with things that shouldn't be standard, like containing coghazards and reality benders. What's the difference between this and a regular locker exactly? Is it lined with telekill or something?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The sudden, perfect understanding of how to manipulate reality was where you lost me. That is too convenient to be readily believable, and there isn't a reason for it to arise in the context of the original anomaly (why don't we see how this has affected the wife? Is she in fact dreading her husband's return, and thereby keeping him away, which would be a nicely ironic ending?). And the newly omnipotent D-Class doesn't indicate that he is using his power to avoid an impending undesirable experience, so the point is kinda lost.

I would have preferred an exploration of the original effect - how has it made the family operate for three months, always thinking it's almost 8pm?

One last nitpick - I'm not convinced that "my ASS" is genuine German colloquialism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The image isn't as interesting now, but at least it's correct, darnit!

And +1. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh. My bad. Fixing.

It's good now, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh, you're right, I had somehow glossed over that. I see what you mean, now.

I think I partially get it now; the clock (and the restructuring of the concept of time) is based off of the wife counting away the minutes that her husband is past curfew.

The rest I don't quite understand… yet. The exponential slowing down of the clock is step 2, perhaps the husband's rejection of the curfew and postponement of coming home.

Basically, I like this SCP, but now the clock hands are bugging me, too. Thanks, TL.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean, that the description says the hands are "stopped at eight o'clock" (and I get the feeling they are, in reality, just ever so slightly before 8:00), but the image shows 8:23 or so. If the point is that the writer of the description has been affected by the anomaly, that could be neat, but on the surface, it just looks like a mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that is the point; curfew is 8, so the clock never quite reaches 8 because that's forever postponed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, though I think the one thing holding me back from an upvote is that the clock hands are patently not at 8 o'clock.

Unless that's the point. <.<
